founded.notes: Formed on 15 March 1892 following a disagreement between Everton directors and John Houlding
founded.notes_recognition: Board of Trade certificate granted on 3 June 1892

club.founded_notes: c. August 1833 – 17 March 1902. English businessman and local politician who created the club after Everton moved to Goodison Park, leaving Anfield empty.

football_league_entry.first_season_achievement: Won league at first attempt, unbeaten in 28 matches (22 wins)

nicknames_notes: The Reds became the official nickname after 1965 when Bill Shankly introduced all-red kit. Kopites refers to fans (from Kop stand). Liverpudlians refers to supporters from Liverpool.

stadium.name: Anfield
stadium.capacity_notes: Expanded to 61,276 following completion of Anfield Road Stand redevelopment in 2024

record_attendance_all_time.match: FA Cup tie vs Wolverhampton Wanderers
record_attendance_all_time.notes: All-time record standing for over 70 years

record_attendance_league.notes: Most recent league record set January 2025

current_management.notes: Won Premier League title in 2024-25 inaugural season; won LMA Manager of the Year award

ownership.notes: FSG also owns Boston Red Sox (MLB), Pittsburgh Penguins (NHL), RFK Racing (NASCAR), and TGL

colors.history_notes: Changed to all-red kit in 1964 under Bill Shankly. Prior to 1896: red shirts & white shorts. From 1896-1964: red shirts & white shorts to differentiate from Everton (all blue)

motto.adoption: Became club anthem circa 1963, added to coat of arms and Shankly Gates (unveiled 1982)
motto.usage: Sung before each home match at Anfield by supporters

major_honors.european_cups_notes: 1977, 1978, 1981, 1984, 2005, 2019. English record (Real Madrid & AC Milan have more overall)
major_honors.fifa_club_world_cup_notes: 2019 (first English side to win international treble that year: Champions League + UEFA Super Cup + Club World Cup)
major_honors.total_major_honors_notes: 52 major men's honours across all competitions

kit_manufacturer.notes: Adidas returns for third tenure (previously 1985-1996, 2006-2012). Ended 13-year Nike partnership.
kit_manufacturer.kit_design_2025_26: Strawberry Red with crisp white accents, cuffed sleeves, simple crewneck

training_ground.melwood_status: Women's first team, youth teams, and academy (re-purchased June 2023)
training_ground.melwood_history: First team training ground from 1950s until November 2020
training_ground.melwood_facilities: Three full-size synthetic pitches, indoor astro, rehabilitation rooms, gymnasium, swimming pool, restaurant, recreational facilities

interesting_facts: Liverpool is the most successful English club in European competitions with 14 international trophies
Record attendance of 61,905 set in 1952 vs Wolverhampton Wanderers - stood untouched for over 70 years
The Kop Stand named after Spion Kop hill in South Africa (Boer War reference from 1899)
In 2019, became first English club to win the international treble: Champions League + UEFA Super Cup + Club World Cup
Arne Slot won Premier League title in inaugural 2024-25 season, winning 20 league titles (joint-record with Manchester United)
Global fanbase of 200+ officially recognized LFC Supporters Clubs in at least 50 countries
One of the world's most valuable and widely supported football clubs
